content.method: Legal-text analysis of the enacted or draft 2026 reform statutes/decrees compared against the pre-2026 funding formula and governance statutes; structured interviews with Klebelsberg Center or Interior Ministry officials and municipal association representatives.
content.question: Does the 2026 tankerület restructuring preserve, modify, or abolish the per-pupil funding penalty structure that creates fiscal pressure on small classes, and does it grant municipalities or principals any formal standing in closure decisions?
content.why_it_matters: Every governance-oriented proposal (T1 presumption-against-closure, T2 reciprocal contracts, T3 grade-split, T4 networked hubs) depends on whether the reformed system retains centralized authority without local standing or creates a genuine legislative vehicle for safeguards. This is foundational to assessing implementation feasibility for T1 and T2 specifically.
